nonBlockingIssues.description: Header summary numbers in miri-compatibility.md (685 pass, 26 ignored, 31 verified) are inconsistent with per-file table totals (893 pass, 27 ignored, 33 non-skipped). The per-file table is internally consistent and appears authoritative.
The 'scene' test binary (47 tests) listed as Clean in the per-file table was not actually run under Miri in either the current or previous session. Low risk since it's pure drawing/layout logic.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update kernel-auditor skill's Miri section to document the per-binary approach: run `cargo +nightly miri test --test <binary> -- --test-threads=1` for each test binary individually, not the blanket full-suite command. The blanket command is impractical for large test suites (exits on first UB per binary, times out).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature description explicitly required per-binary approach. Worker correctly followed it. Current skill text says 'run cargo +nightly miri test -- --test-threads=1' (blanket command) which doesn't work at scale.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to kernel-auditor skill: Miri timeout handling guidance — set 120s timeout per binary, skip binaries that exceed it, use #[cfg_attr(miri, ignore)] for incompatible tests, and use cfg(miri) to reduce iteration counts in stress tests (e.g., 10 iterations under Miri vs 1000 normally).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker had to discover these patterns independently. adversarial_stress and adversarial_vma were correctly skipped for timeout. scheduler_state already uses cfg(miri) iteration reduction. These patterns should be in the skill for future workers.
